justification: All 7 business rules identified with correct classifications. Refund window (BR-7) correctly marked invariant: false. Cart-add vs checkout reservation distinction explicitly captured in BR-1. Discount/promotion mutual exclusivity captured in BR-4. Multiple ambiguities surfaced (discount priority, pre-delivery refund, per-item vs per-order refund window, timer start point). 10 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria provided, well exceeding the 4 minimum. Minor note: BR-2 (15-minute timeout) marked invariant: false, which is debatable but reasonably justified as a policy threshold rather than a state integrity rule.

observations.text: All 7 required rules present with strong justifications for each invariant classification
BR-7 refund window correctly classified as invariant: false with clear reasoning about policy vs state integrity
BR-1 explicitly states cart-add does NOT reserve inventory — the key domain distinction
Rich ambiguity surfacing: discount priority, pre-delivery refunds, per-item vs per-order refund window, day-30 boundary inclusivity
10 acceptance criteria in proper Given/When/Then format, covering all major rules
Escalations include concrete options and recommendations, not just flags
Edge cases are thorough — includes race conditions (inventory drop during checkout, payment at 15:01)

BR-2 (15-minute timeout) marked invariant: false — arguably this is an inventory management invariant since without it reservations could lock inventory indefinitely, though the specific duration being policy is a reasonable interpretation
Confidence level of 55 is well-calibrated given the material ambiguities identified
